questions.question_text: What is the official distance of the Marathon race?
questions.explanation: The official distance of a marathon is 26 miles and 385 yards (42.195 km). This standard was established in 1908 at the London Olympics[reference:0].

questions.question_text: Eritrea, which became the 182nd member of the UN in 1993, is in the continent of
questions.explanation: Eritrea is a country located in the Horn of Africa region of Eastern Africa. It became the 182nd member of the United Nations on May 28, 1993[reference:1].

questions.question_text: Which Indian scientist has the honor of receiving not only the Nobel Prize but also the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: Dr. C. V. Raman, a renowned Indian physicist, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930 for his pioneering work on the scattering of light (Raman Effect) and was later conferred the Bharat Ratna in 1954[reference:2][reference:3].

questions.question_text: Which of the following continents is called the Continent of Disparities?
questions.explanation: Europe is often referred to as the "Continent of Disparities" due to the significant economic and social differences between its various regions. While some parts have high levels of service coverage, other areas lag far behind[reference:4].

questions.question_text: Among the following India is the largest exporter of
questions.explanation: India is the world's largest exporter of granite, with exports valued at over $718 million in 2022[reference:5].

questions.question_text: Which of the following islands is known as the 'Paradise of the Pacific'?
questions.explanation: The Hawaiian Islands are famously described as the "Paradise of the Pacific" due to their stunning natural beauty, tropical climate, and idyllic landscapes[reference:6].

questions.question_text: Which is the only Continent through which the Tropic of Cancer, the Equator and the Tropic of Capricorn pass?
questions.explanation: Africa is the only continent that the Tropic of Cancer, the Equator, and the Tropic of Capricorn all pass through. This makes it unique in terms of its latitudinal coverage[reference:7].

questions.question_text: Match the following:
options: a - i, b - iii, c - ii, d - iv
a - ii, b - iv, c - i, d - v
a - iii, b - i, c - iv, d - ii
a - iii, b - iv, c - ii, d - i
questions.correct_answer: a - iii, b - iv, c - ii, d - i
questions.explanation: Japan is known as the 'Land of the Rising Sun', Norway is known as the 'Land of the Midnight Sun', Finland is known as the 'Land of Thousand Lakes', and Bhutan is known as the 'Land of Thunderbolt' (or 'Land of the Thunder Dragon')[reference:8].

questions.question_text: How does the Constitution of India describe India?
questions.explanation: Article 1 of the Constitution of India declares that 'India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States'. The constituent assembly deliberately chose this term to emphasize the indestructible nature of the nation[reference:9][reference:10].

questions.question_text: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act was enacted in the year ______
questions.explanation: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA), now known as the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), was enacted in 2005. It was officially notified on September 5, 2005, but the question specifically asks for the enactment year[reference:11].

questions.question_text: After the general elections, the Protem Speaker is
options: appointed by the President of India
appointed by the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court
the seniormost member of the Lok Sabha
questions.correct_answer: appointed by the President of India
questions.explanation: The Protem Speaker of the Lok Sabha is appointed by the President of India. Their main duty is to administer oaths to newly elected members and facilitate the election of the full-time Speaker and Deputy Speaker[reference:12][reference:13].

questions.question_text: Who had given the idea to have a Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: Manabendra Nath Roy (M.N. Roy) first proposed the idea of a Constituent Assembly to draft the Constitution of India in 1934[reference:14][reference:15].

questions.question_text: In which part of the Constitution of India, are the Fundamental Duties enshrined?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties are enshrined in Part IV-A (Article 51A) of the Constitution of India. They were added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976[reference:16].

questions.question_text: The Constitution gives the powers of superintendence over all subordinate courts to the High Court's under
questions.explanation: Article 227 of the Constitution of India empowers every High Court to exercise superintendence over all courts and tribunals within its territorial jurisdiction[reference:17].

questions.question_text: The members of the Rajya Sabha are elected by
options: the elected members of the Legislative Assembly
the elected members of the Legislative Council
questions.correct_answer: the elected members of the Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: Members of the Rajya Sabha are elected by the elected members of the State Legislative Assemblies through a system of proportional representation with the single transferable vote[reference:18]【15L14-L15】.

questions.question_text: Who has the right to transfer any case anywhere in India?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India has the power to transfer criminal cases or appeals from one High Court to another, or from a subordinate court in one State to a subordinate court in another State, to ensure the ends of justice are served[reference:19].

questions.question_text: What are the demerits of a Local Government?
options: Elections of the Gram Sabha are not held regularly
Most State Governments have not transferred significant powers to local Governments
questions.explanation: Local governments in India face several challenges, including irregular elections, inadequate devolution of powers by state governments, and a lack of sufficient financial resources[reference:20][reference:21].

questions.question_text: A steady increase in the general level of prices as a result of excessive increase in aggregate demand 'as compared to aggregate supply' is termed as
questions.explanation: Demand-pull inflation occurs when aggregate demand in an economy outpaces aggregate supply, leading to a general increase in the price level. This is often described as 'too much money chasing too few goods'【19†L0-L4】.

test_name: Test 2 - Bihar Police Constable Memory Based Paper (1st Oct 2023 Shift 2)

questions.question_text: Which of the following does not wet the walls of the glass vessel in which it is kept?
questions.explanation: Mercury is a liquid metal with high surface tension and does not wet glass surfaces, unlike water, alcohol, or phenol which spread on glass.

questions.question_text: During a change in temperature of a substance, in which form is the supplied heat energy stored?
options: Both kinetic and potential energy form
questions.explanation: During a temperature change, the heat energy supplied is stored as internal energy (kinetic energy of molecules). However, during a phase change, it is stored as potential energy. Since the question specifies 'change in temperature' (not phase change), the heat increases kinetic energy, but the option 'Heat energy form' is commonly accepted in basic science.

questions.question_text: When the driver of a moving bus suddenly applies brakes, the passengers sitting in it fall in the forward direction. This can be explained by:
questions.explanation: Newton's first law (law of inertia) states that a body in motion continues in motion unless acted upon by an external force. Passengers tend to continue moving forward when the bus stops suddenly.

questions.question_text: 'सखि वे मुझसे कहकर जाते' is a poetic line by which poet?
questions.explanation: The line 'Sakhi ve mujhse kahkar jaate' is a famous composition by the renowned Hindi poet Maithilisharan Gupt, often called the 'Rashtrakavi'.

questions.question_text: Choose the correct sequence: (i) The Prime Minister holds the most important position among MPs. (ii) MPs come through elections. (iii) India has a parliamentary system. (iv) The Prime Minister is elected by Lok Sabha MPs.
questions.explanation: Logical sequence: First, India has a parliamentary system (iii). Then, MPs come through elections (ii). Among them, the PM holds the most important position (i), and the PM is elected by Lok Sabha MPs (iv).

questions.question_text: Select the correct spelling:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'तकनीकी' which means technical.

questions.question_text: Select the correct synonym: 'रमा'
questions.explanation: 'रमा' is another name for Goddess Lakshmi, and 'इन्दिरा' is also a name for Lakshmi. Both are synonyms.

questions.question_text: Select the synonym: 'वण्य'
questions.explanation: 'वण्य' means describable or worthy of description. 'वर्णन योग्य' is its correct synonym.

questions.question_text: Select the antonym: 'उत्कर्ष'
questions.explanation: 'उत्कर्ष' means progress or excellence. Its antonym is 'अपकर्ष' meaning decline or deterioration.

questions.question_text: The time between evening and night is called:
questions.explanation: 'गोधूलि' (Godhuli) refers to the twilight period, literally 'cow dust hour' - the time when cows return home at dusk.

questions.question_text: Select the odd one out:
questions.explanation: रोष (anger), कोष (dictionary/treasury), अमर्ष (intolerance) are related to emotions or concepts, while अशोक (Ashoka - a tree or emperor's name) is different.

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank: ______ इस गुणा में गया, कालकवलित हो गया।
questions.explanation: The correct word is 'जो' (who/which), making the sentence: 'जो इस गुणा में गया, कालकवलित हो गया' (Whoever went into this multiplication became time-eaten/obsolete).

questions.question_text: Select the incorrect part of the sentence: पिताजी मुझको नई कमीज [A] / और माँ के लिए [B] / एक सुन्दर साड़ी लाए। [C] / कोई त्रुटि नहीं [D]
questions.explanation: The error is in part [A] 'मुझको नई कमीज' - the correct form should be 'मुझे नई कमीज' (using the correct case).

questions.question_text: Select the correct option to complete the sentence: The bag was heavy, but he managed to ______ it up the stairs.
questions.explanation: The context of moving something heavy upstairs using effort - 'pulled' is appropriate. However, the original answer key indicated option 1 for this question, which corresponds to 'pulled'.

questions.question_text: Convert to indirect speech: He said, 'Let Hari come with us, mother. I'll take care of him.'
options: He requested his mother to let Hari come with them as he will take care of him
He informed his mother to let Hari come with them as he would take care of him
He told his mother to let Hari come of with them as he would take care of him
He told to his mother let Hari come with us as he would take care of him
questions.correct_answer: He requested his mother to let Hari come with them as he would take care of him
questions.explanation: The correct indirect speech: 'He requested his mother to let Hari come with them as he would take care of him.' The verb 'said' changes to 'requested' (since it's a proposal), 'us' changes to 'them', and 'will' changes to 'would'.

questions.question_text: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: All options appear identical in text, but the correct spelling is 'Fabulous' (F-a-b-u-l-o-u-s). The answer key indicates option 1 is correct.

questions.question_text: Fill up the blank: Kanishka was initiated ______ Buddha by Buddhist monks.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is 'into' - 'initiated into' means formally admitted or introduced to a belief or practice.

questions.question_text: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment: I must replying this letter immediately.
questions.explanation: The correct form is 'reply to this letter'. 'Reply' is used with 'to' when indicating the object of the reply. 'Replying' is incorrect after the modal 'must'.

questions.question_text: Select the option closest in meaning to 'through thick and thin': I will always be grateful because she has been with me through thick and thin.
options: in your own place and other places
in both good times and bad times
over a long period of time
questions.correct_answer: in both good times and bad times
questions.explanation: 'Through thick and thin' means to persist through all kinds of circumstances, both favorable and unfavorable.

questions.question_text: Frame a question tag to get the answer: 'He has gone to the railway station to see off his cousin.'
questions.explanation: A question tag for this statement would be 'hasn't he?' None of the given wh-words form a proper tag. The answer key indicates 'None of the above'.

questions.question_text: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word:
questions.explanation: The given word appears to be missing in the text, but based on answer key, the synonym is 'Raise' (likely for a word like 'elevate' or 'lift').

questions.question_text: Centre - State relations in India are dependent upon: (i) constitutional provisions (ii) conventions and practices (iii) judicial interpretations (iv) mechanics for dialogue
questions.explanation: Centre-State relations in India are shaped by constitutional provisions, established conventions and practices, judicial interpretations by the Supreme Court, and various dialogue mechanisms like the Inter-State Council and NITI Aayog.

questions.question_text: The concept of sovereign parliament was given by which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of a sovereign or supreme parliament originated in England, where the doctrine of parliamentary sovereignty (or parliamentary supremacy) holds that Parliament has absolute legal authority.

questions.question_text: How many spokes are there in the Ashoka Wheel of the Indian National Flag?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra (Wheel) in the Indian National Flag has 24 spokes, representing the 24 hours of the day and the 24 virtues mentioned in Buddhist philosophy.

questions.question_text: What is the meaning of 'fraternity' in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Fraternity in the Indian Constitution means a sense of brotherhood and fellow-feeling among all Indians, ensuring dignity of the individual and unity of the nation.

questions.question_text: Subjects like computer software come under which list?
questions.explanation: Computer software was not originally mentioned in any of the three lists. Under Article 248, any subject not mentioned in State or Concurrent lists falls under the Residuary list (Union jurisdiction).

questions.question_text: Which Article deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Article 44 is specifically about the Uniform Civil Code (one of the DPSPs). However, the question may intend to ask about DPSP generally (Part IV from Articles 36-51). But based on the answer key, Article 44 is the correct choice among given options.

questions.question_text: Prime Minister, Central Cabinet Ministers, Chief Ministers, and Council of Ministers are all members of:
questions.explanation: The National Development Council (NDC) includes the Prime Minister, Union Cabinet Ministers, Chief Ministers of all states, and members of the Planning Commission (now NITI Aayog). It was established to promote cooperative federalism.

questions.question_text: What is the tenure of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India?
questions.explanation: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India holds office for a term of 6 years or until the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is true? (i) Indian Constitution provides three types of municipalities. (ii) CAG is appointed by the Prime Minister. (iii) Schedule 11 deals with languages. (iv) President is elected for 5 years.
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is true: three types - Nagar Panchayat, Municipal Council, Municipal Corporation. Statement (iv) is true. Statement (ii) is false: CAG is appointed by the President. Statement (iii) is false: Schedule 11 deals with Panchayati Raj (languages are in Schedule 8).

questions.question_text: Which historian called Din-i-Ilahi, propounded by Akbar, as a religion?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl, the court historian of Akbar and author of Akbarnama, describes Din-i-Ilahi as a religious order or a Sufistic path.

questions.question_text: Who was the last independent ruler of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The last independent ruler of Bengal before the British was Siraj-ud-Daulah. None of the given options (Dharmapala - Pala dynasty, Rampala - Pala, Ballal Sen - Sena dynasty) were the last independent ruler.

questions.question_text: The Independence of Bangladesh was declared in the year:
questions.explanation: Bangladesh declared independence from Pakistan on March 26, 1971, leading to the Liberation War. It achieved victory on December 16, 1971.

questions.question_text: The Opium War was held between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The First Opium War (1839-1842) was fought between China (Qing Dynasty) and Great Britain (British East India Company) over trade imbalances and the opium trade.

questions.question_text: Whom did the people of Indus Valley Civilization worship?
questions.explanation: The Pashupati seal found at Mohenjo-Daro depicts a seated figure surrounded by animals, identified by scholars as a proto-Shiva or 'Pashupati' (Lord of Animals).

questions.question_text: Who among the following saints was active in the region of Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Eknath (1533-1599) was a Marathi saint, poet, and scholar of the Varkari tradition, active in Maharashtra. Nammalvar was in Tamil Nadu, Surdas in Uttar Pradesh, Ramananda in North India.

questions.question_text: Which mountain volcano buried Pompeii city with ash in 79 AD?
questions.explanation: Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD, burying the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash and pumice.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the breeding site for Gharials in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The Gandak River in Bihar is known as a significant breeding site for Gharials (Gavialis gangeticus), a critically endangered crocodilian species.

questions.question_text: Which of the following companies has secured first rank in Best Industry Category at 4th National Water Awards?
questions.explanation: NTPC Barauni (Bihar) won the first rank in the 'Best Industry' category at the 4th National Water Awards for its water conservation efforts.

questions.question_text: Who among the following personalities of Bihar has been awarded with Padma Shri, 2023?
questions.explanation: Subhadra Devi, a folk artist from Bihar (known for Mithila painting), was awarded the Padma Shri in 2023.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the Patna - Ranchi Vande Bharat Express is/are correct?
options: It is the first Vande Bharat train to run in Bihar
It was flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on 28th June, 2023 from Ranchi railway station
questions.explanation: The Patna-Ranchi Vande Bharat Express was flagged off on June 28, 2023, by PM Modi from Ranchi. It was the first Vande Bharat train to run in Bihar.

questions.question_text: What was the GNPA ratio of Scheduled Commercial Banks in March, 2023?
questions.explanation: As per RBI data, the Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) ratio of Scheduled Commercial Banks declined to 3.9% in March 2023 from 5.9% in March 2022, due to improved asset quality.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the cause of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: Poverty in India is caused by multiple factors including low capital formation, inadequate infrastructure, population pressure, unemployment, and social factors.

questions.question_text: Sweet potato is an underground structure which stores food. Where is the food prepared in this plant?
questions.explanation: Food (carbohydrates) is prepared in the leaves of sweet potato through photosynthesis, then transported and stored in the tuberous roots.

questions.question_text: Opening and closing of stomata is a function performed by:
questions.explanation: Guard cells, specialized cells surrounding the stomatal pore, regulate opening and closing of stomata by changing their turgor pressure in response to environmental conditions.

questions.question_text: Red rot of sugarcane is caused by:
questions.explanation: Red rot of sugarcane is caused by the fungus Colletotrichum falcatum (also known as Glomerella tucumanensis). It causes significant yield losses in sugarcane.

questions.question_text: The term 'plasma membrane' was given by:
questions.explanation: The term 'plasma membrane' was given by J. Q. Plowe in 1931. Earlier terms included 'plasmalemma' by Nageli.

questions.question_text: Crop insurance schemes include:
options: Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)
questions.explanation: Both PMFBY and RWBCIS are major crop insurance schemes implemented by the Government of India to protect farmers against crop loss.

questions.question_text: What is correct about the popular idea of 'Triple Bottom Line'?
options: Promoting higher growth with lower inflation and wages
Promoting higher growth with stable inflation and exchange rates
Generating social and environmental goods together with money profits by corporate houses
Promoting higher export growth without cutting import subsidies
questions.correct_answer: Generating social and environmental goods together with money profits by corporate houses
questions.explanation: The Triple Bottom Line (TBL) concept, coined by John Elkington, focuses on three Ps: Profit (economic), People (social), and Planet (environmental). Businesses should generate social and environmental benefits alongside profits.

questions.question_text: Which of the following cities will set up India's first e-waste recycling unit?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is set to host India's first state-of-the-art e-waste recycling unit, established by the Karnataka government in collaboration with private partners.

questions.question_text: India and five ASEAN countries (Cambodia, Laos-PDR, Myanmar, Thailand, Vietnam) form:
questions.explanation: Mekong-Ganga Cooperation (MGC) is an initiative by India and five ASEAN countries (Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, Vietnam) for cooperation in tourism, culture, education, and transport.

questions.question_text: Which of the following taxes is not a direct tax?
questions.explanation: Sales tax is an indirect tax as it is levied on goods and services and collected from consumers by sellers, who then pay it to the government. Direct taxes are paid directly by the taxpayer (e.g., income tax, wealth tax, gift tax, estate duty).

questions.question_text: Consider statements about Dairy Cooperatives: (i) 2% loan interest subvention (ii) 100% vaccination for FMD and brucellosis (iii) Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Fund with ₹15,000 crore
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct as per government schemes: interest subvention for dairy co-ops, 100% vaccination target against FMD and brucellosis, and the Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund of ₹15,000 crore.

questions.question_text: UNDP prepares:
questions.explanation: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) publishes the annual Human Development Index (HDI) report, measuring countries based on life expectancy, education, and per capita income.

questions.question_text: According to new Public Sector Enterprise (PSE) Policy (2021), which statements are true or false? (i) Strategic sector includes atomic energy, space, defence, transport, telecom, power, petroleum, coal, banking, insurance. (ii) Minimum one Government company will be kept; remaining merged/privatized/shutdown.
questions.explanation: The 2021 PSE policy classified strategic sectors. Statement (i) is true (though the list is broad). Statement (ii) is false: In strategic sectors, there will be a maximum of four PSEs, not necessarily one, and remaining may be privatized/merged.

questions.question_text: Choose the correct answer: (i) Modern Indian coins are token coins. (ii) Cupronickel metal is used to discourage melting. (iii) Coinage Act, 2011 prohibits melting of coins.
questions.explanation: All statements are correct: Modern Indian coins are token coins (face value > metal value). Cupronickel is used to prevent melting for profit. The Coinage Act, 2011 prohibits melting or destruction of coins.

questions.question_text: Find the letter series which follows the rule: 'The number of letters skipped in between the adjacent letters in the series are multiples of 3.'
questions.explanation: In AELPZ: A to E skip 3 letters (B,C,D - 3 letters skipped), E to L skip 6 letters (F,G,H,I,J,K - 6, multiple of 3), L to P skip 3 letters (M,N,O - 3), P to Z skip 9 letters (Q,R,S,T,U,V,W,X,Y - 9, multiple of 3).

questions.explanation: Based on the pattern in the figure (not shown here, but answer key indicates 9 as the correct missing character).

questions.question_text: J, H, Q, S, X, L, I sitting in a row facing south. Two persons between J and Q. X sits between J and S and second to right of H. L second to left of Q and immediate left of I. Q and H at extreme ends. Who sits third to the left of J?
questions.explanation: Arranging based on conditions: Q and H are ends. L is second to left of Q, immediate left of I. X is second to right of H. J and Q have two persons between. The final order (south-facing) gives Q as third to left of J.

questions.question_text: The angle of depression of the top of a tower to a point A is 60°. If the point is 80 m away from the foot of the tower, then the height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: tan(60°) = height/distance => √3 = h/80 => h = 80√3 m. The angle of depression from top to point equals angle of elevation from point to top.

If the sum of areas of two circles with radii R1 and R2 equals the area of a circle of radius R, then:
questions.explanation: Area sum: πR1² + πR2² = πR² => R1² + R2² = R².

questions.question_text: The average age of 5 boys is 16 years. Average of 4 boys is 16 years 3 months. Age of 5th boy is:
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 = 5×16 = 80 years. Sum of 4 = 4×16.25 = 65 years. So 5th boy = 80 - 65 = 15 years.

questions.question_text: 729 ml of mixture contains milk and water in ratio 7:2. How much more water to add to get ratio 7:3?
questions.explanation: Milk = (7/9)×729 = 567 ml, water = 162 ml. New ratio 7:3 means water = (3/7)×milk = (3/7)×567 = 243 ml. Extra water = 243 - 162 = 81 ml.

questions.question_text: If roots of x² - bx + c = 0 are α and β, then equation with roots -α and -β is:
questions.explanation: Sum of new roots = -α-β = -(α+β) = -b. Product = (-α)(-β) = αβ = c. So equation: x² - (sum)x + product = x² - (-b)x + c = x² + bx + c = 0.

questions.question_text: Graphically, the pair of equations 6x - 3y + 10 = 0 and 2x - y + 9 = 0 represent two lines which are:
questions.explanation: First equation: 6x-3y+10=0 => 2x-y+10/3=0. Second: 2x-y+9=0. Both have same coefficients for x and y (2 and -1), but different constants => parallel lines.

questions.question_text: 1/8 part of pencil is black, 1/2 of remaining is white. Remaining blue part is 3½ cm. Length of pencil?
questions.explanation: Let total length = L. Black = L/8, remaining = 7L/8. White = 1/2 of remaining = 7L/16, remaining blue = 7L/16 = 3.5 cm => L = 8 cm.

questions.question_text: Animals of which phylum have jointed legs?
questions.explanation: Phylum Arthropoda is characterized by jointed appendages (legs), exoskeleton, and segmented bodies. Examples: insects, spiders, crustaceans.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is called 'Vaccinia' in Latin?
questions.explanation: 'Vaccinia' is the Latin term for cowpox. It was used by Edward Jenner to develop the first smallpox vaccine (from Latin 'vacca' meaning cow).

questions.question_text: Evolutionary history of an organism is known as:
questions.explanation: Phylogeny is the evolutionary history and relationships among species or groups of organisms. Ontogeny is the development of an individual organism.

questions.question_text: Wings of pigeons, mosquitoes and bats show:
questions.explanation: Wings of birds (pigeons), insects (mosquitoes), and mammals (bats) are analogous structures - they evolved independently for similar function (flight) due to convergent evolution.

questions.question_text: Bacilli are bacteria which are:
questions.explanation: Bacilli are rod-shaped bacteria. Other shapes: cocci (spherical), spirilla (spiral), vibrio (comma-shaped).

questions.question_text: What are the edible sea fishes?
questions.explanation: Katla and Rohu are freshwater fish (river fish). Hilsa, Pomfret, and Mackerel are marine/sea fishes commonly consumed.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is the hardest metal?
questions.explanation: Tungsten has the highest tensile strength and is the hardest metal among common metals. It has a Mohs hardness of about 7.5.

questions.question_text: Which of the following metals does not form amalgam with mercury?
questions.explanation: Iron does not form an amalgam with mercury. Most other metals like zinc, copper, magnesium, gold, silver do form amalgams.

questions.question_text: Alcohol derived from fermentation of germinated barley grains is known as:
questions.explanation: Beer is produced by fermenting germinated barley grains (malt) with water, hops, and yeast. Wine is from grapes, vodka from grains/potatoes, rum from sugarcane.

questions.question_text: A radioactive substance has a half-life of four months. Three-fourth of the substance would decay in:
questions.explanation: After one half-life (4 months), 1/2 remains. After two half-lives (8 months), 1/4 remains, so 3/4 decayed.

questions.question_text: When Eno salt is added to water, bubbles of which gas are released?
questions.explanation: Eno salt contains sodium bicarbonate and citric acid. When mixed with water, they react to produce carbon dioxide (CO2) gas, causing effervescence.

questions.question_text: Among the following, which element was first produced artificially?
questions.explanation: Technetium (atomic number 43) was the first element to be artificially produced in 1937 by Carlo Perrier and Emilio Segrè. It has no stable isotopes.

questions.question_text: Which of the following elements does not contain neutrons?
questions.explanation: The most common isotope of hydrogen (protium) has 1 proton and 0 neutrons. Deuterium has 1 neutron, tritium has 2 neutrons.

questions.question_text: Which of the following gases is used as tear gas?
questions.explanation: Chlorine gas (Cl2) was historically used as a chemical weapon and tear gas. However, modern tear gases use compounds like CS gas. Among given options, Cl2.

questions.question_text: A polymer used for making non-stick surface coating for utensils is:
questions.explanation: Teflon (polytetrafluoroethylene - PTFE) is used for non-stick coatings on cookware due to its low friction and high heat resistance.

questions.question_text: Which of the following cities are associated with ship building? (i) Antwerp (ii) Trieste (iii) Minsk (iv) Hamburg
questions.explanation: Antwerp (Belgium), Trieste (Italy), and Hamburg (Germany) are major shipbuilding centers. Minsk (Belarus) is landlocked and not associated with shipbuilding.

questions.question_text: Who among the following is known as the 'land of red river and blue hills'?
questions.explanation: Assam is known as the 'Land of Red River and Blue Hills' - the Red River refers to the Brahmaputra (which turns red during certain seasons) and Blue Hills refer to the blue-hued hills of the region.

questions.question_text: Which statements about India's Antarctic mission are correct? (i) First base: Dakshin Gangotri (ii) Now used as supply base and transit camp (iii) Maitri station manned throughout the year
questions.explanation: All statements are correct. Dakshin Gangotri (1983) was India's first station, now a supply base. Maitri (1989) is India's active research station manned year-round. Bharti is another station.

questions.question_text: The Arctic region and the Antarctica continent are situated near:
questions.explanation: The Arctic region surrounds the North Pole, and Antarctica surrounds the South Pole. They are at opposite ends of the Earth.

questions.question_text: Match the following: List I (Railway Zones) - List II (Headquarters): A. West Central, B. South East Central, C. East Central, D. North Eastern
questions.explanation: West Central - Jabalpur (1), South East Central - Bilaspur (3), East Central - Hajipur (4), North Eastern - Gorakhpur (2).

questions.question_text: The first Union Territory of India to run 100% on solar energy is:
questions.explanation: Diu (Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu UT) became the first UT to run 100% on solar energy, meeting its daytime electricity demand through solar power.

questions.question_text: Consider statements about Puducherry: (i) Bounded by Bay of Bengal on east and Tamil Nadu on other three sides. (ii) Consists of four small unconnected districts.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true. Puducherry UT comprises four unconnected districts: Puducherry, Karaikal, Yanam, and Mahe, located in three different states but administered together.

questions.question_text: An important crop of commercial grain cultivation is:
questions.explanation: Wheat is a major commercial grain crop. Sugarcane, cotton, and jute are commercial crops but not grain crops. The question specifies 'commercial grain cultivation'.

questions.question_text: The laws of reflection hold true for:
questions.explanation: The laws of reflection (angle of incidence = angle of reflection, incident ray, reflected ray, normal lie in same plane) are valid for all reflecting surfaces - plane, concave, convex.

questions.question_text: Which is a better conductor?
questions.explanation: Among the given options, chromium has the highest electrical conductivity. Nichrome is an alloy with lower conductivity (designed for heating elements).

questions.question_text: The image formed by an astronomical telescope is:
questions.explanation: An astronomical telescope produces a virtual, inverted, and magnified image of distant objects, as the final image is formed at infinity (or near point) for relaxed viewing.

questions.question_text: Loudness of sound is measured in terms of:
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound is directly proportional to the square of the amplitude of the sound wave. Frequency determines pitch, not loudness.

questions.question_text: On which technique does a Photostat machine work?
questions.explanation: Photostat machines (and modern photocopiers) use xerography, which is based on electrostatic image making - using light, photoconductive drums, and toner to create copies.

questions.question_text: One Astronomical Unit (AU) is the average distance between:
questions.explanation: One Astronomical Unit (AU) is approximately 149.6 million km, the average distance from the Earth to the Sun.

test_name: Test 3 - Bihar Police Constable Memory Based Paper (Memory 2)

questions.question_text: Which of the following does not wet the walls of the glass vessel in which it is kept?
questions.explanation: Mercury has high surface tension and cohesive forces stronger than adhesive forces with glass, so it does not wet glass surfaces. Water, alcohol, and phenol wet glass easily.

questions.question_text: During a change in temperature of a substance, in which form is the supplied heat energy stored?
options: Both kinetic and potential energy form
questions.explanation: During a temperature change (not phase change), heat increases the kinetic energy of molecules. However, the stored energy is still referred to as 'heat energy' or internal energy in basic science contexts.

questions.question_text: When the driver of a moving bus suddenly applies brakes, the passengers sitting in it fall in the forward direction. This can be explained by:
questions.explanation: Newton's first law (inertia) states that a body in motion continues in motion unless acted upon by an external force. Passengers continue moving forward when the bus stops suddenly.

questions.question_text: 'सखि वे मुझसे कहकर जाते' is a poetic line by which poet?
questions.explanation: This famous line is from the Hindi poet Maithilisharan Gupt, often called the 'Rashtrakavi' (National Poet).

questions.question_text: Choose the correct sequence: (i) The Prime Minister holds the most important position among MPs. (ii) MPs come through elections. (iii) India has a parliamentary system. (iv) The Prime Minister is elected by Lok Sabha MPs.
questions.explanation: Logical order: India has a parliamentary system → MPs come through elections → Prime Minister holds the most important position → PM is elected by Lok Sabha MPs.

questions.question_text: Select the correct spelling:
questions.explanation: The correct Hindi spelling for 'technical' is 'तकनीकी'. The other options have incorrect consonant combinations.

questions.question_text: Select the correct synonym: 'रमा'
questions.explanation: 'रमा' and 'इन्दिरा' are both names of Goddess Lakshmi, making them synonyms.

questions.question_text: Select the synonym: 'वण्य'
questions.explanation: 'वण्य' means 'describable' or 'worthy of description'. 'वर्णन योग्य' is its exact synonym.

questions.question_text: Select the antonym: 'उत्कर्ष'
questions.explanation: 'उत्कर्ष' means progress or excellence; its antonym is 'अपकर्ष' meaning decline or deterioration.

questions.question_text: The time between evening and night is called:
questions.explanation: गोधूलि (Godhuli) refers to the twilight period, literally 'cow dust hour' - when cows return home at dusk.

questions.question_text: Select the odd one out:
questions.explanation: रोष (anger), कोष (dictionary/treasury), अमर्ष (intolerance) are abstract nouns or concepts; अशोक (Ashoka - tree or emperor) is different.

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank: ______ इस गुणा में गया, कालकवलित हो गया।
questions.explanation: 'जो' (who/which) correctly completes the sentence: 'जो इस गुणा में गया...' (Whoever went into this multiplication...).

questions.question_text: Select the incorrect part of the sentence: पिताजी मुझको नई कमीज [A] / और माँ के लिए [B] / एक सुन्दर साड़ी लाए। [C] / कोई त्रुटि नहीं [D]
questions.explanation: Part [A] 'मुझको नई कमीज' is incorrect. The correct form should be 'मुझे नई कमीज' (using the proper case).

questions.question_text: Complete the sentence: The bag was heavy, but he managed to ______ it up the stairs.
questions.explanation: 'Pulled' is the most appropriate verb for moving something heavy upward with effort. The answer key confirms option 1.

questions.question_text: Convert to indirect speech: He said, 'Let Hari come with us, mother. I'll take care of him.'
options: He requested his mother to let Hari come with them as he will take care of him
He informed his mother to let Hari come with them as he would take care of him
He told his mother to let Hari come of with them as he would take care of him
He told to his mother let Hari come with us as he would take care of him
questions.correct_answer: He requested his mother to let Hari come with them as he would take care of him
questions.explanation: The imperative 'Let...' becomes a request. 'Us' changes to 'them', 'will' to 'would', and 'said' becomes 'requested'.

questions.question_text: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is F-a-b-u-l-o-u-s. All options appear identical in the text, but the answer key indicates the first option as correct.

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank: Kanishka was initiated ______ Buddha by Buddhist monks.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is 'into' - 'initiated into' means formally admitted to a belief or practice.

questions.question_text: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment: I must replying this letter immediately.
questions.explanation: After modal 'must', use base verb 'reply'. 'Reply' takes the preposition 'to' before the object.

questions.question_text: Select the option closest in meaning to 'through thick and thin':
options: in your own place and other places
in both good times and bad times
over a long period of time
questions.correct_answer: in both good times and bad times
questions.explanation: 'Through thick and thin' means persisting through all circumstances, both favorable and unfavorable.

questions.question_text: Frame a question tag to get the answer: 'He has gone to the railway station to see off his cousin.'
questions.explanation: A proper tag would be 'hasn't he?' None of the given wh-words form a valid tag. Answer key confirms 'None of the above'.

questions.question_text: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: (word missing in PDF, but answer key indicates)
questions.explanation: Based on the answer key, the synonym is 'Raise' (likely for a word like 'elevate' or 'lift').

questions.question_text: Centre-State relations in India are dependent upon: (i) constitutional provisions (ii) conventions and practices (iii) judicial interpretations (iv) mechanics for dialogue
questions.explanation: Centre-State relations are shaped by constitutional provisions, conventions, judicial interpretations, and dialogue mechanisms like the Inter-State Council.

questions.question_text: The concept of sovereign parliament was given by which country?
questions.explanation: The doctrine of parliamentary sovereignty originated in England, where Parliament has absolute legal authority.

questions.question_text: How many spokes are there in the Ashoka Wheel of the Indian National Flag?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra has 24 spokes, representing the 24 hours of the day and 24 virtues in Buddhist philosophy.

questions.question_text: What is the meaning of 'fraternity' in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Fraternity means a sense of brotherhood and fellow-feeling among all Indians, ensuring the dignity of the individual and national unity.

questions.question_text: Subjects like computer software come under which list?
questions.explanation: Computer software was not originally mentioned in any list; under Article 248, it falls under the Residuary list (Union jurisdiction).

questions.question_text: Which Article deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Article 44 specifically deals with the Uniform Civil Code, one of the Directive Principles. (Part IV contains Articles 36-51, but among given options, 44 is correct.)

questions.question_text: Prime Minister, Central Cabinet Ministers, Chief Ministers, and Council of Ministers are all members of:
questions.explanation: The NDC includes the PM, Union Cabinet Ministers, Chief Ministers, and Planning Commission (now NITI Aayog) members.

questions.question_text: What is the tenure of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India?
questions.explanation: The CAG holds office for 6 years or until the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is true? (i) Indian Constitution provides three types of municipalities. (ii) CAG is appointed by the Prime Minister. (iii) Schedule 11 deals with languages. (iv) President is elected for 5 years.
questions.explanation: (i) True: Nagar Panchayat, Municipal Council, Corporation. (iv) True: President's term is 5 years. (ii) False: CAG appointed by President. (iii) False: Schedule 11 is Panchayati Raj; languages are Schedule 8.

questions.question_text: Which historian called Din-i-Ilahi, propounded by Akbar, as a religion?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl, author of Akbarnama, described Din-i-Ilahi as a religious order or Sufistic path.

questions.question_text: Who was the last independent ruler of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The last independent ruler was Siraj-ud-Daulah, not among the given options. The options listed are from earlier dynasties (Pala, Sena).

questions.question_text: The Independence of Bangladesh was declared in the year:
questions.explanation: Bangladesh declared independence on March 26, 1971, leading to the Liberation War, which ended on December 16, 1971.

questions.question_text: The Opium War was fought between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The First Opium War (1839-1842) was fought between China (Qing Dynasty) and Great Britain.

questions.question_text: Whom did the people of Indus Valley Civilization worship?
questions.explanation: The Pashupati seal found at Mohenjo-Daro depicts a proto-Shiva figure, identified as 'Pashupati' (Lord of Animals).

questions.question_text: Which saint was active in the region of Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Eknath (1533-1599) was a Marathi saint-poet of the Varkari tradition, active in Maharashtra.

questions.question_text: Which volcano buried Pompeii with ash in 79 AD?
questions.explanation: Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD, burying Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash.

questions.question_text: Which river is the breeding site for Gharials in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The Gandak River in Bihar is a significant breeding ground for the critically endangered Gharial (Gavialis gangeticus).

questions.question_text: Which company secured first rank in 'Best Industry' at 4th National Water Awards?
questions.explanation: NTPC Barauni (Bihar) won the first rank in the Best Industry category at the 4th National Water Awards.

questions.question_text: Which personality from Bihar was awarded Padma Shri in 2023?
questions.explanation: Subhadra Devi, a Mithila painting artist from Bihar, received the Padma Shri in 2023.

questions.question_text: Which statements about Patna-Ranchi Vande Bharat Express are correct?
options: It is the first Vande Bharat train to run in Bihar
It was flagged off on 28th June, 2023 from Ranchi
questions.explanation: The Patna-Ranchi Vande Bharat Express, flagged off on June 28, 2023, was indeed the first Vande Bharat train in Bihar.

questions.question_text: What was the GNPA ratio of Scheduled Commercial Banks in March 2023?
questions.explanation: According to RBI data, the Gross NPA ratio of SCBs declined to 3.9% in March 2023 from 5.9% in March 2022.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a cause of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: Poverty in India is multi-causal: low capital formation, inadequate infrastructure, population pressure, unemployment, etc.

questions.question_text: Sweet potato stores food underground. Where is the food prepared?
questions.explanation: Food is synthesized in the leaves via photosynthesis and then transported to the tuberous roots for storage.

questions.question_text: Opening and closing of stomata is performed by:
questions.explanation: Guard cells regulate stomatal opening and closing by changing turgor pressure in response to environmental cues.

questions.question_text: Red rot of sugarcane is caused by:
questions.explanation: Colletotrichum falcatum (Glomerella tucumanensis) is the fungal pathogen causing red rot in sugarcane.

questions.question_text: The term 'plasma membrane' was given by:
questions.explanation: The term 'plasma membrane' was coined by J. Q. Plowe in 1931. Nageli used the term 'plasmalemma' earlier.

questions.question_text: Crop insurance schemes include:
options: Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)
questions.explanation: PMFBY and RWBCIS are two major crop insurance schemes implemented by the Government of India.

questions.question_text: What is correct about the 'Triple Bottom Line'?
options: Promoting higher growth with lower inflation and wages
Promoting higher growth with stable inflation and exchange rates
Generating social and environmental goods together with money profits by corporate houses
Promoting higher export growth without cutting import subsidies
questions.correct_answer: Generating social and environmental goods together with money profits by corporate houses
questions.explanation: The Triple Bottom Line (Profit, People, Planet) requires businesses to generate social and environmental benefits along with financial profits.

questions.question_text: Which city will set up India's first e-waste recycling unit?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is set to host India's first state-of-the-art e-waste recycling facility.

questions.question_text: India and five ASEAN countries (Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, Vietnam) form:
questions.explanation: MGC is the cooperative initiative between India and these five ASEAN nations for tourism, culture, education, and transport.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is not a direct tax?
questions.explanation: Sales tax is an indirect tax (collected by sellers from consumers), whereas estate duty, gift tax, and wealth tax are direct taxes.

questions.question_text: Consider statements about Dairy Cooperatives: (i) 2% loan interest subvention (ii) 100% vaccination for FMD and brucellosis (iii) Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Fund with ₹15,000 crore
questions.explanation: All three are correct government schemes: interest subvention to dairy co-ops, 100% vaccination target, and the AHIDF of ₹15,000 crore.

questions.question_text: UNDP prepares:
questions.explanation: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) publishes the annual Human Development Index (HDI).

questions.question_text: According to PSE Policy 2021: (i) Strategic sector includes atomic energy, space, defence, transport, telecom, power, petroleum, coal, banking, insurance. (ii) Minimum one Government company will be kept; remaining merged/privatized/shutdown.
questions.explanation: (i) is true (broad list of strategic sectors). (ii) is false: in strategic sectors, a maximum of four PSEs will be kept, not necessarily one.

questions.question_text: Which statements are correct? (i) Modern Indian coins are token coins. (ii) Cupronickel discourages melting. (iii) Coinage Act 2011 prohibits melting of coins.
questions.explanation: All statements are correct: token coins (face value > metal value), cupronickel prevents melting for profit, and the Act prohibits coin destruction.

questions.question_text: Find the letter series where skipped letters between adjacent letters are multiples of 3:
questions.explanation: A→E (skip 3), E→L (skip 6), L→P (skip 3), P→Z (skip 9). All skips are multiples of 3.

questions.explanation: After arranging (Q _ _ J _ _ H or reverse with conditions), Q ends up third to the left of J.

questions.question_text: Average age of 5 boys = 16 years. Average of 4 = 16 years 3 months. Age of 5th?
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 = 80 years. Sum of 4 = 4 × 16.25 = 65 years. 5th = 80-65 = 15 years.

questions.question_text: 729 ml mixture milk:water = 7:2. How much water to add for 7:3?
questions.explanation: Milk = 567 ml, water = 162 ml. For 7:3, water = (3/7)×567 = 243 ml. Add 243-162 = 81 ml.

questions.question_text: Roots of x² - bx + c = 0 are α,β. Equation with roots -α, -β is:
questions.explanation: Sum = -(α+β) = -b, product = αβ = c. Equation: x² - (sum)x + product = x² + bx + c = 0.

questions.question_text: 6x-3y+10=0 and 2x-y+9=0 represent lines that are:
questions.explanation: Both have slope 2 (first: 6x-3y+10=0 ⇒ y=2x+10/3; second: y=2x+9). Different intercepts ⇒ parallel.

questions.question_text: 1/8 of pencil black, 1/2 of remaining white. Blue part = 3.5 cm. Pencil length?
questions.explanation: Let length = L. Black = L/8, remaining = 7L/8. White = 7L/16, blue = 7L/16 = 3.5 ⇒ L = 8 cm.

questions.question_text: Animals of which phylum have jointed legs?
questions.explanation: Arthropods are characterized by jointed appendages (legs, antennae, etc.).

questions.question_text: Which disease is called 'Vaccinia' in Latin?
questions.explanation: 'Vaccinia' is Latin for cowpox, from which the first smallpox vaccine was derived (vacca = cow).

questions.question_text: Evolutionary history of an organism is known as:
questions.explanation: Phylogeny is the evolutionary history and relationships among species. Ontogeny is individual development.

questions.question_text: Wings of pigeons, mosquitoes, and bats show:
questions.explanation: These are analogous structures that evolved independently for flight, indicating convergent evolution.

questions.question_text: Bacilli are bacteria which are:
questions.explanation: Bacillus refers to rod-shaped bacteria. Coccus = spherical, spirillum = spiral, vibrio = comma-shaped.

questions.question_text: Which are edible sea fishes?
questions.explanation: Katla and Rohu are freshwater fish. Hilsa, Pomfret, and Mackerel are marine fish.

questions.question_text: Which is the hardest metal?
questions.explanation: Tungsten has the highest tensile strength and Mohs hardness (7.5) among these options.

questions.question_text: Which metal does not form amalgam with mercury?
questions.explanation: Iron does not amalgamate with mercury. Zinc, copper, and magnesium do form amalgams.

questions.question_text: Alcohol from fermented germinated barley is:
questions.explanation: Beer is produced by fermenting malted (germinated) barley with water, hops, and yeast.

questions.question_text: Half-life = 4 months. Time for 3/4 of substance to decay?
questions.explanation: After 4 months: 1/2 remains. After 8 months: 1/4 remains ⇒ 3/4 decayed.

questions.question_text: When Eno salt is added to water, which gas bubbles out?
questions.explanation: Eno contains sodium bicarbonate and citric acid, which react to produce carbon dioxide (CO₂).

questions.question_text: Which element was first produced artificially?

questions.question_text: Polymer used for non-stick coating on utensils:
questions.explanation: Teflon (PTFE) is used for non-stick cookware due to its low friction and high heat resistance.

questions.question_text: Which cities are associated with shipbuilding? (i) Antwerp (ii) Trieste (iii) Minsk (iv) Hamburg
questions.explanation: Antwerp, Trieste, and Hamburg are major shipbuilding ports. Minsk is landlocked.

questions.question_text: Which place is known as 'land of red river and blue hills'?
questions.explanation: Assam is called the 'Land of Red River (Brahmaputra) and Blue Hills'.

questions.question_text: Correct statements about India's Antarctic mission: (i) First base: Dakshin Gangotri (ii) Now used as supply base (iii) Maitri manned year-round
questions.explanation: All are correct: Dakshin Gangotri was first (1983), now a supply base; Maitri (1989) is active year-round.

questions.question_text: Arctic and Antarctica are situated near:
questions.explanation: Arctic surrounds the North Pole; Antarctica surrounds the South Pole.

questions.question_text: Match: West Central, South East Central, East Central, North Eastern with their headquarters.
questions.explanation: West Central → Jabalpur (1), South East Central → Bilaspur (3), East Central → Hajipur (4), North Eastern → Gorakhpur (2).

questions.question_text: First UT to run 100% on solar energy:
questions.explanation: Diu (part of DNH&DD) became the first UT to be 100% solar-powered for daytime electricity.

questions.question_text: Statements about Puducherry: (i) Bounded by Bay of Bengal and Tamil Nadu (ii) Four unconnected districts.
questions.explanation: Both are true: Puducherry is on the Bay of Bengal, surrounded by Tamil Nadu on three sides, and consists of four unconnected districts.

questions.question_text: Important crop of commercial grain cultivation:
questions.explanation: Wheat is a major commercial grain crop. Sugarcane, cotton, and jute are commercial but not grains.

questions.question_text: Laws of reflection hold true for:
questions.explanation: The laws of reflection are universal for all types of reflecting surfaces.

questions.explanation: Chromium has higher electrical conductivity than nickel, nichrome, and iron.

questions.question_text: Image formed by an astronomical telescope is:
questions.explanation: The final image is virtual, inverted, and magnified when viewing distant objects.

test_name: Test 4 - Bihar Police Constable Memory Based Paper (16 July 2025)

questions.question_text: Where did Exercise Varuna 2025, the 23rd edition of the India-France bilateral naval exercise, take place?
questions.explanation: Exercise Varuna 2025 was conducted in the Arabian Sea from March 19-22, 2025. The Arabian Sea is strategically important for both nations due to its sea lanes and oil trade routes.

questions.question_text: From which space center did Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla begin his ascent to the International Space Station on 25 June 2025?
questions.explanation: He launched from Launch Complex 39A at Kennedy Space Center, Florida, USA. The mission marked the second time an Indian went to space and the first Indian to live aboard the ISS.

questions.question_text: Which river intersects the Tropic of Cancer twice?
questions.explanation: The Mahi River is unique among Indian rivers as it crosses the Tropic of Cancer twice. It flows through Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Gujarat before draining into the Arabian Sea.

questions.question_text: Which river is known as the 'sorrow of Bihar'?
questions.explanation: The Kosi River is called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' because of its frequent, devastating floods that cause extensive damage to life, property, and agriculture.

questions.question_text: Utkal Divas, also known as Odisha Foundation Day, is celebrated annually on which date?
questions.explanation: Utkal Divas is celebrated on April 1 to mark the formation of Odisha as a separate province in 1936, carved out of the Bihar and Orissa Province.

questions.question_text: Which religion has the highest literacy in Bihar (as per 2011 Census)?
questions.explanation: Jains have the highest literacy rate in Bihar at 94.1%, followed by Christians (80.3%) and Buddhists (72.7%).

questions.question_text: Which day is celebrated as World Ozone Day?
questions.explanation: World Ozone Day is celebrated on 16th September to mark the anniversary of the Montreal Protocol (1987) for protecting the ozone layer.

questions.question_text: Who represented the Congress in the second round table conference held in London?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi attended the Second Round Table Conference in 1931 after the Gandhi-Irwin Pact, as the sole representative of the Indian National Congress.

questions.question_text: Karachi session of Indian National Congress was held in 1931. It was presided over by:
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel presided over the 1931 Karachi session of the Indian National Congress.

questions.question_text: In which year was the third battle of Panipat fought?

questions.question_text: In which state is Kanha Tiger Reserve located?

questions.question_text: In which year was Delhi officially announced as the Capital of British India by Emperor George V?
questions.explanation: In December 1911, at the Delhi Durbar, Emperor George V announced the shifting of the capital from Calcutta to Delhi.

questions.question_text: Where did Mahatma Gandhi first apply the Satyagraha movement in India?

questions.explanation: Gandhi returned to India from South Africa in 1915 after spending 21 years there fighting for civil rights and developing Satyagraha.

questions.question_text: Where is the Niagara river found?

questions.question_text: A soap bubble and a liquid drop both have the same surface tension and radius. Which will have more excess pressure inside it?
questions.explanation: Excess pressure inside a soap bubble = 4S/R, while for a liquid drop = 2S/R. The bubble has double the excess pressure.

questions.question_text: The ________ regulates and controls the amount of light entering the eye.
questions.explanation: The pupil's size is controlled by the iris, but the pupil itself regulates light entry. According to the answer key, 'pupil' is correct.

questions.question_text: The substance that is usually lost by the body during dehydration is:
questions.explanation: Dehydration leads to loss of water and electrolytes, primarily sodium chloride (salt).

questions.question_text: What is the meaning of the idiom 'कान खड़े होना' (kaan khade hona)?
options: To experience all kinds of things
questions.explanation: The idiom means 'to become alert or cautious' (सचेत होना).

questions.question_text: Who was the teacher of Sheikh Nizamuddin Auliya?
questions.explanation: Sheikh Nizamuddin Auliya was the disciple of Baba Farid (Fariduddin Ganjshakar) of the Chishti order.

questions.question_text: Coal mines are located in which district of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Singrauli district in Madhya Pradesh is known for its major coal mines.

questions.question_text: Which Fundamental Right prohibits trafficking, forced labour, and children working under 14 years of age?
questions.explanation: Articles 23 and 24 (Right against Exploitation) prohibit human trafficking, forced labour, and child labour under 14.

questions.question_text: The Ashok Mehta committee is related to:
questions.explanation: The Ashok Mehta Committee (1977) was formed to review the Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question_text: The central idea of the passage (about earthquakes) is:
options: earthquakes and the damage they cause
questions.correct_answer: earthquakes and the damage they cause
questions.explanation: The passage describes the power and destruction caused by earthquakes.

questions.question_text: What is the growth rate of the Service Sector in India in 2019-20?
questions.explanation: The service sector growth rate in India for 2019-20 was approximately 7%.

questions.question_text: If A = [[1, x], [0, 1]], then what is Aⁿ? (n is any natural number)
questions.explanation: For a matrix of the form [[1, x], [0, 1]], its nth power is [[1, nx], [0, 1]].

questions.question_text: Who can remove a Judge of a High Court?
options: Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister
The President on the advice of the Chief Justice of India
The President on the resolution of parliament passed by a special majority
questions.correct_answer: The President on the resolution of parliament passed by a special majority
questions.explanation: A High Court judge can be removed by the President only after an address by each House of Parliament supported by a special majority.

questions.question_text: Who was the Chairman of the inaugural session of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha was the temporary chairman (pro tem) of the first meeting of the Constituent Assembly on 9 December 1946.

questions.question_text: What is the permanent emotion (sthāyī bhāva) of Karuna Rasa?
questions.explanation: In Indian aesthetics, the permanent emotion of Karuna Rasa (pathos) is sorrow.

questions.question_text: The marginal propensity to consume lies between ________.
questions.explanation: MPC is between 0 and 1 because people spend a fraction of additional income, not more than the whole or negative.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between vertical and horizontal reservation?
questions.explanation: Vertical reservation applies to categories like SC/ST/OBC; horizontal reservation applies within vertical categories (e.g., women, disabled).

questions.question_text: Who first used the word 'biodiversity'?
questions.explanation: The term 'biodiversity' was coined by Walter G. Rosen in 1986.

questions.question_text: Who is the author of the book 'A Passage to England'?
questions.explanation: 'A Passage to England' is a book by Nirad C. Chaudhuri.

questions.question_text: Where was the United Nations Charter signed by 51 original members?
questions.explanation: The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945 at the San Francisco Conference.

questions.question_text: What is the term of the Estimates Committee of the Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Estimates Committee is constituted annually for a term of one year.

questions.question_text: At which place did Mahatma Gandhi first start his Satyagraha in India?
questions.explanation: Champaran Satyagraha (1917) was Gandhi's first civil disobedience movement in India.

questions.question_text: Which scheme was launched in Bihar for women's free-of-cost abortion care from accredited private clinics?
options: Mukhyamantri Jhuqgi Jhodi Mahila Saksharta Yojana
questions.explanation: Yukti Yojana provides free abortion care to women at accredited private clinics in Bihar.

questions.question_text: The type of hybridization and the number of lone pair(s) of electrons of Xe in XeOF₄ are respectively:
questions.explanation: Xe in XeOF₄ has sp³d² hybridization with square pyramidal geometry and one lone pair.

questions.question_text: A notice should always be:
options: Formal, brief and to the point
Formal and direct but never factual
questions.correct_answer: Formal, brief and to the point
questions.explanation: Notices are formal, concise, and direct, providing key information clearly.

questions.question_text: On which date was a Naval Central Strike Committee elected in order to deal with the rising strikes by Indian sailors of the RIN?
questions.explanation: The Naval Central Strike Committee was elected on 19 February 1946 during the Royal Indian Navy mutiny.

questions.question_text: Which of the following diseases is not caused by mosquitoes?
questions.explanation: Plague is caused by Yersinia pestis bacteria transmitted by fleas, not mosquitoes.

questions.question_text: The red colour of tomato is due to the presence of which compound?
questions.explanation: Lycopene is a carotenoid pigment responsible for the red colour of tomatoes.

questions.question_text: If x and y are natural numbers and x + y = 2, then:
questions.explanation: Natural numbers are positive integers; the only solution to x + y = 2 is x=1, y=1, so x=y.

questions.question_text: 'The old man kicked the bucket.' The sentence means:
options: The old man stole the bucket
The old man lifted the bucket
The old man befooled the visitor
questions.explanation: 'Kicked the bucket' is an idiom meaning to die.

questions.question_text: If n resistors each with R Ω as resistance are connected in parallel, then the equivalent resistance of the network is:
questions.explanation: For n equal resistors in parallel, equivalent resistance = R/n.

test_name: Test 6 - Bihar Police Constable Previous Year Paper 2017

questions.question_text: What is the feminine form of the word 'कवि' (kavi - poet)?
questions.explanation: The feminine form of 'kavi' is 'kaviyatri'.

questions.question_text: Polio and smallpox are caused by:
questions.explanation: Polio is caused by poliovirus, and smallpox was caused by variola virus (both viral diseases).

questions.question_text: Which is the largest state of India by area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest Indian state by area (342,239 sq km).

questions.question_text: Water transport in plants occurs through:
questions.explanation: Xylem tissue transports water and minerals from roots to shoots.

questions.question_text: Find the correct adverb form of 'Affection'.
questions.explanation: The adverb is 'affectionately' (e.g., She spoke affectionately).

questions.question_text: What type of bond is present in methane (CH₄)?
questions.explanation: Methane has four single covalent bonds between carbon and each hydrogen.

questions.question_text: Find the plural form of the word 'Thief'.
questions.explanation: The plural of 'thief' is 'thieves' (changing -f to -ves).

questions.question_text: A person has 2000 rupees. If he spends 300 rupees, what percentage does he spend?
If an item is bought for 1000 rupees and sold for 1200 rupees, what is the profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit = 200 rupees, profit % = (200/1000)×100 = 20%.

questions.question_text: Which of the following island groups is located in the Arabian Sea?
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep is in the Arabian Sea; Andaman-Nicobar is in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question_text: When a moving bus suddenly stops, passengers fall forward. This is due to which of Newton's laws?
questions.explanation: Newton's first law (inertia) explains why passengers continue moving forward when the bus stops.

questions.question_text: Yam bought a bicycle for 1000 rupees and sold it for 1300 rupees. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit = 300 rupees, profit % = (300/1000)×100 = 30%.

questions.question_text: Which of the following contains citric acid in abundance?
questions.explanation: Citrus fruits like lemon are rich in citric acid.

questions.question_text: What is the synonym of 'परिधि' (paridhi)?
questions.explanation: परिधि means circumference/perimeter; its synonym is परिमाप.

questions.question_text: Which of the following rivers flows into the Arabian Sea?
questions.explanation: The Narmada flows westwards and drains into the Arabian Sea.

questions.question_text: At what Celsius temperature is the Fahrenheit reading five times the Celsius reading?
If the length of a simple pendulum is doubled, what is the effect on its time period?
questions.explanation: T ∝ √L, so if L doubles, T becomes √2 times.

questions.question_text: X-rays are used in the treatment of which type of disease?
questions.explanation: X-rays are used in radiotherapy for cancer treatment, as well as for imaging.

questions.question_text: The minimum possible temperature below which a substance cannot be cooled is:
questions.explanation: Absolute zero is -273.15°C or 0 Kelvin.

questions.question_text: A person standing in front of a fire receives maximum heat by:
questions.explanation: Heat from a fire is primarily transferred by radiation (infrared).

questions.question_text: Cooking gas (LPG) contains more than 90% of:
questions.explanation: LPG consists mainly of butane and propane, with butane often exceeding 90%.

questions.question_text: Which acid causes sourness in milk?
questions.explanation: Milk turns sour due to the production of lactic acid by bacteria.

questions.question_text: What is the percentage of pure gold in 18 carat gold?
questions.explanation: 24 carat is pure gold (100%). 18 carat is (18/24)×100 = 75% pure gold.
